justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Jurisdiction-specific rules identified for all 3 jurisdictions — CA §226 wage statement requirements, DE works council co-determination gate, ID contractor classification with distinct fee terminology. (2) Germany works council approval explicitly identified as a hard deployment gate/pre-condition (BR-4, AC-2). (3) Driver classification data model difference clearly identified — employee (CA/DE) vs contractor (ID) with distinct labeling requirements (BR-5, AC-4). (4) Formal escalation for legal review present across 4 detailed escalations covering deduction terminology, real-time definition, AB5/Prop 22 status, and Betriebsrat timeline. (5) No single global acceptance criteria set — ACs are jurisdiction-specific throughout. (6) Confidence level is 52, well below the 60 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on all three jurisdictions — California §226 statutory penalties quantified, Germany BetrVG §87(1)(6) cited precisely, Indonesia misclassification risk identified as a data model and labeling concern
Correctly identified the subtle trap: contractor terminology mimicking employee wage statements creates misclassification litigation risk (BR-5)
Works council approval modeled as a hard deployment gate with a feature flag assertion (AC-2), not just a soft mention
Driver relocation edge case (ID→CA) captures the reclassification data model challenge — historical records retain contractor terminology while new records switch to employee terminology
AB5/Prop 22 escalation shows deep legal awareness — correctly notes the constitutional challenge is under appeal and recommends the conservative path
Works council conditions modeled as potentially reshaping the feature (BR-8, AC-3), not just a binary approve/deny gate — shows understanding of co-determination scope
Phased rollout recommendation embedded in escalation 4 — build CA and ID first, DE only after Betriebsrat conditions are known
Confidence reasoning is well-justified, citing specific gaps (exact deduction categories, Betriebsrat conditions, Prop 22 status) rather than generic uncertainty

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
